briefs.title: NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview

metadata.summary_en: NASA’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24, will deliver approximately 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. This mission will be the second flight of the Cygnus XL, the larger, more cargo-capable version of the company’s solar-powered spacecraft.

secondary.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight

At its farthest point, crew inside the Orion spacecraft will have traveled about 252,760 […]

main.title: Artemis II astronauts break a record, name a crater

metadata.summary_en: A few minutes before 2PM ET on Monday, the crew of Artemis II broke a record set 56 years ago by the ill-fated Apollo 13 mission - at over 248,655 miles, they have now traveled farther from Earth than any humans before them. They marked the occasion with a crater naming ceremony that left the […]
